title: 13 AFT Notice of Change Transactions on ORS
body_text:
13	AFT Notice of Change Transactions on ORS
13.1	Overview of AFT Notice of Change on ORS
AFT Notice of Change transactions are used to notify AFT Originators (via the Originating Direct Clearers) of changes to your financial institution customerâs Transit Number or account number. Once the Originator updates this information, future AFT transactions will post correctly to the customerâs account. AFT Notice of Change transactions are non-financial transactions that involve the exchange of information only.
13.2	Creating AFT Notice of Change Transactions on ORS
Proceed as follows to enter an AFT Notice of Change transaction on ORS:
Log in to ORS and locate the AFT transaction for which you want to create a Notice of Change transaction. See Section 7.5, Searching for AFT Transactions on ORS.
Select the
N
link in the A column. See Figure 1.
Note:	The N link is also available on the Search View page.
Figure 1: Search Results Page
Complete the Edit AFT Notice of Change page as follows. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Edit AFT Notice of Change
â
NOC Transit
â To update the Transit Number associated with the AFT transaction, select a new Transit Number from the drop-down list.
â
NOC Account
â To update the account number associated with the AFT transaction, enter a new account number in the text box (maximum 14 digits).
Note:	Fields that are left blank retain the original information.
Select
Submit
. The Search View or Search Results page opens depending on the page from which the N link was selected. The AFT Notice of Change status is displayed in the N column. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: AFT Notice of Change Status
P
= Pending
C
= Complete
13.3	Editing AFT Notice of Change Transactions on ORS
ORS users can only edit AFT Notice of Change transactions with an N column status of Pending (P). If the N column has a status of Complete (C), the item has been sent to the originating financial institution and the N link in column A isnât available.
Proceed as follows to edit an AFT Notice of Change transaction on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Summary Option
Select the
N
link in the A column beside the AFT Notice of Change transaction that you want to edit. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Return Summary â AFT
Note:	The N link is also available from the Search Results
or Search View
page.
Make the necessary changes (e.g., enter a different account number).
Select
Submit
.
13.4	Cancelling AFT Notice of Change Transactions on ORS
Users can only cancel AFT Notice of Change transactions with an N column status of Pending (P). If the N column has a status of Complete (C), the item has been sent to the originating financial institution and the N link in column A isnât available.
Proceed as follows to cancel an AFT Notice of Change transaction on ORS:
Log in to ORS and select the
Summary
link on the ORS menu bar. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Summary Option
Select the
N
link in the A column beside the AFT Notice of Change transaction that you want to delete. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Return Summary â AFT
Note:	The N link is also available from the Search Results
or Search View
pages.
Select
Delete
.
END
